title: Acoridium maleolens
taxonomy: Acoridium maleolens is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Acoridium, which is a small group of orchids found in tropical regions. Acoridium maleolens is an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces, such as trees, for support. The classification of Acoridium maleolens places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae, a large and diverse group of orchids. Further classification identifies it as belonging to the tribe Aeridinae, which is a group of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids.

The health benefits of Acoridium maleolens include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine.
